utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Next, verify if each is fully specified with all required details. For health_coverage, the format demands physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan provides physical_health and mental_health plans with costs, plus extra executive protection items with costs—this satisfies the requirement. For property_coverage, the format requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides home_contents_insurance with plan and cost; this meets the requirement. For travel_coverage, the format requires travel insurance with costs. The plan includes multiple travel insurance entries with plans and costs, satisfying the requirement. For additional_services, the format requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists two services with one-time costs, satisfying the requirement. For total_budget, the format requires a complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides monthly_cost and one_time_cost; it lacks an annual figure but does provide a breakdown component (monthly and one-time). Since the format says monthly/annual, having monthly is acceptable as a complete breakdown element; thus it is reasonably specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each plan or service named in the final coverage plan to the corresponding item in the ground-truth ratings. For health physical and mental plans, I used the primary_health_coverage sections. For property, I used property_protection.home_content. For travel, I used mobility_coverage.travel_insurance. For enhancement and general services, I used the respective sections with exact names.
